heading: Sealing permitted by Aleuts, Eskimos, and Indians
label: 1153 Sealing permitted by Aleuts, Eskimos, and Indians
notes: Amendments1983— amended section generally, substituting provisions authorizing sealing by Aleuts, Eskimos, and Indians, which were previously contained in , for provisions authorizing scientific research on fur seal resources. See .Pub. L. 98–129section 1152 of this titlesection 1154 of this title
amendments: Amendments1983— amended section generally, substituting provisions authorizing sealing by Aleuts, Eskimos, and Indians, which were previously contained in , for provisions authorizing scientific research on fur seal resources. See .Pub. L. 98–129section 1152 of this titlesection 1154 of this title

children.label: a
children.text: Indians, Aleuts, and Eskimos who dwell on the coasts of the North Pacific Ocean are permitted to take fur seals and dispose of their skins after the skins have been officially marked and certified by a person authorized by the Secretary: , That the seals are taken for subsistence uses as defined in , and only in canoes not transported by or used in connection with other vessels, and propelled entirely by oars, paddles, or sails, and manned by not more than five persons each, in the way hitherto practiced and without the use of firearms. This authority shall not apply to Indians, Aleuts, and Eskimos while they are employed by any person for the purpose of taking fur seals or are under contract to deliver the skins to any person.Providedsection 1379(f)(2) of this title

children.label: b
children.text: Indians, Aleuts, and Eskimos who live on the Pribilof Islands are authorized to take fur seals for subsistence purposes as defined in , under such conditions as recommended by the Commission and accepted by the Secretary of State pursuant to regulations promulgated by the Secretary.section 1379(f)(2) of this title
